title: `*dockerClient.getBearerToken` does not retry
body:
### Issue Description

Just saw this error:

```console
$ podman pull quay.io/openshift-release-dev/ocp-v4.0-art-dev@sha256:0a6c7b7dc54f27d25e53c43364773e6513869ea9d6b15fad2e9393d31332f9b0
Trying to pull quay.io/openshift-release-dev/ocp-v4.0-art-dev@sha256:0a6c7b7dc54f27d25e53c43364773e6513869ea9d6b15fad2e9393d31332f9b0...
Error: initializing source docker://quay.io/openshift-release-dev/ocp-v4.0-art-dev@sha256:0a6c7b7dc54f27d25e53c43364773e6513869ea9d6b15fad2e9393d31332f9b0: Requesting bearer token: invalid status code from registry 500 (Internal Server Error)
```

It looks like this is handled by `*dockerClient.getBearerToken`, which might do a retry when getting http 500.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
 - not sure that can be reproduced without server side error injection.

### Describe the results you received

See above

### Describe the results you expected

A retry when getting internal server error.

comments.body:
My general position is that retries deep in libraries are a design mistake; top-level callers should be able to drive retries on their own schedule and policy, without lower-level retries introducing unavoidable delays and latency. Hence `getBearerToken` should not be retrying, and this should be handled in a ~top-level c/common/pkg/retry.

Now, as to this specific failure…
- I’m not sure that retrying on HTTP status 500 a good idea in general. AFAICT we don’t know what is going on inside the server, a retry might make things worse for the site. I don’t have enough expertise/experience here.
  - (OTOH `c/image/docker.bodyReader` did give up on the judgement to an extent, allowing one “free” reconnection even in situations where there wasn’t much hope. Maybe c/common/pkg/retry should have something like that, classifying errors into “never retry / always retry / retry, maybe, once”.
- … from a quick look it seems that c/image doesn’t _allow_ detecting HTTP status 500, it returns a string error without a specific type. That might be worth changing anyway.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I got same problem

```
root@at800-9000a2:~# podman pull ascendhub.huawei.com/public-ascendhub/ascend-pytorch:23.0.RC3-1.11.0-centos7
Trying to pull ascendhub.huawei.com/public-ascendhub/ascend-pytorch:23.0.RC3-1.11.0-centos7...
Error: initializing source docker://ascendhub.huawei.com/public-ascendhub/ascend-pytorch:23.0.RC3-1.11.0-centos7: Requesting bear token: invalid status code from registry 500 (Internal Server Error)
```

and the network is fine

```
root@at800-9000a2:~# ping ascendhub.huawei.com
PING 19a1c3ecdc554e09878a2b78d47afb8f.vip1.huaweicloudwaf.com (116.205.146.43) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from ecs-116-205-146-43.compute.hwclouds-dns.com (116.205.146.43): icmp_seq=1 ttl=48 time=29.6 ms
64 bytes from ecs-116-205-146-43.compute.hwclouds-dns.com (116.205.146.43): icmp_seq=2 ttl=48 time=30.4 ms
64 bytes from ecs-116-205-146-43.compute.hwclouds-dns.com (116.205.146.43): icmp_seq=3 ttl=48 time=29.5 ms
```
